rationale: The only resume change swapped lesser-known employers (re:cinq, Optilyz, GISCollective) for prestigious Western ones (Google, Meta, Amazon), yet the score stayed identical at 4/10. Both evaluations reach the same conclusions for the same reasons: insufficient professional C++ experience, no cloud compute/distributed-systems leadership, and no track record mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers. The wording differs slightly ('brief C++ exposure in early roles' vs 'only mentioned in academic/volunteer context'), but this is ordinary run-to-run rephrasing rather than a substantive shift, and notably the variant did NOT credit the famous employer names. No reasoning keys off the changed employer names.
